chapter_title.ar: حَدِيثُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام) مَعَ يَزِيدَ
chapter_title.en: Hadith of Ali Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> with Yazeed<sup>la</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 236)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 236)
text: كُنْتُ عِنْدَهُ وَ سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَجِي‏ءُ مِنْهُ الشَّيْ‏ءُ عَلَى حَدِّ الْغَضَبِ يُؤَاخِذُهُ اللَّهُ بِهِ فَقَالَ اللَّهُ أَكْرَمُ مِنْ أَنْ يَسْتَغْلِقَ عَبْدَهُ وَ فِي نُسْخَةِ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ (عليه السلام) يَسْتَقْلِقَ عَبْدَهُ.

en.hubeali: H 14808 – Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ali Bin Atiyya, who has said:

Abu Abdullahasws said, ‘I was in hisasws presence and a man asked himasws about a man from whom something comes out due to his anger, will Allahazwj Hold him responsible for it?’ So heasws said: ‘Allahazwj is more benevolent than Tying down Hisazwj servant’. And in a copy from Abu Al-Hassan The Firstasws: (which says Allahazwj does not) ‘Coerce Hisazwj servant’.
en.sarwar: 361. Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ali Bin Atiyya, who has said: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said, ‘I was in his (a.s.) presence and a man asked him (a.s.) about a man from whom something comes out due to his anger, will Allah (azwj) Hold him responsible for it?’ So he (a.s.) said: ‘Allah (azwj) is more benevolent than Tying down His (azwj) servant’. And in a copy from Abu Al-Hassan The First (a.s.): (which says Allah (azwj) does not) ‘Coerce His (azwj) servant’.
